> <WhiteQuality:Burst>
> After being played, gain [Arg1] Temporary <PowerIconKeywords:STR>, and engraved cards also benefit from this <PowerIconKeywords:STR> buff.
the burst orison
ah yes, the *bane of my fucking existence*
the cn term for orison is quite literally "engraving" so
it would probably be the best verb choice to use for orisons
not looking forward to solving *that* problem.
what problem?
Reformatting how Burst is written.

My current plan is to implement a clarification between when a card is *played* and when its effects *resolve*.
Would help in this case as I can specify something along the lines of `Gain 18 Temporary STR before resolving this card's effects.`
But would also be useful in cases like Liz Exalt. `Discard all of Liz's cards, and resolve each of their effects.`
fortunately for me this game doesn't have "The Stack", so in most cases *playing* a card and *resolving* a card don't need to be distinguished at all.
but the fact that Burst exists means that that distinction does in fact exist within the game's coding, at least to some small extent.
anyways I need sleep, gngn lovelies
order matters for various effects yeah
like blade of the titan applies the 60% critdmg buff after the exalt finishes casting
i.e. exalts don't benefit from the crit damage buff
which is...kind of a big deal and I might edit the description to mention that explicitly, actually
